`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/collapsible.tsx` (lines 5-7) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/tooltip.tsx` (lines 20-21) #### Pattern 2: Components with Styling via `cn()` Utility **Purpose:** Merge custom classes with Tailwind defaults. ```typescript function AccordionTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: AccordionPrimitive.Trigger.Props) { return ( {children} ) } ``` **Files:** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/accordion.tsx` (lines 28-54) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/dialog.tsx` (lines 42-79) #### Pattern 3: Orientation Support (Horizontal/Vertical) Components supporting orientation use `data-orientation` attributes and data-driven CSS: ```typescript function Tabs({ className, orientation = "horizontal", ...props }: TabsPrimitive.Root.Props) { return ( ) } ``` This allows child components to respond via scoped group selectors: ``` group-data-horizontal/tabs:flex-col group-data-vertical/tabs:w-full group-data-vertical/tabs:justify-start ``` **Files:** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/tabs.tsx` (lines 8-24) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/separator.tsx` (lines 7-23) #### Pattern 4: CVA (Class Variance Authority) for Variants Complex components use CVA for managing multiple style variants: ```typescript const buttonVariants = cva( "group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-md border border-transparent bg-clip-padding text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none select-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-3 focus-visible:ring-ring/50", { variants: { variant: { default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/80", outline: "border-border bg-background shadow-xs hover:bg-muted", secondary: "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80", ghost: "hover:bg-muted hover:text-foreground", destructive: "bg-destructive/10 text-destructive hover:bg-destructive/20", link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline", }, size: { default: "h-9 gap-1.5 px-2.5", xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),8px)] px-2 text-xs", sm: "h-8 gap-1 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10px)] px-2.5", lg: "h-10 gap-1.5 px-2.5", icon: "size-9", }, }, defaultVariants: { variant: "default", size: "default", }, } ) function Button({ className, variant = "default", size = "default", ...props }: ButtonPrimitive.Props & VariantProps) { return ( ) } ``` **Files:** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/button.tsx` (lines 8-60) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/tabs.tsx` (lines 26-54) #### Pattern 5: Positioner + Portal Pattern (For Floating Components) Complex components use a three-layer structure for positioning: ```typescript function PopoverContent({ className, align = "center", alignOffset = 0, side = "bottom", sideOffset = 4, ...props }: PopoverPrimitive.Popup.Props & Pick< PopoverPrimitive.Positioner.Props, "align" | "alignOffset" | "side" | "sideOffset" >) { return ( ) } ``` **Files:** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/popover.tsx` (lines 16-48) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/select.tsx` (lines 59-99) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` (lines 21-52) #### Pattern 6: Render Prop Pattern Some sub-components accept a `render` prop to compose complex children: ```typescript function DialogContent({ className, children, showCloseButton = true, ...props }: DialogPrimitive.Popup.Props & { showCloseButton?: boolean }) { return ( {children} {showCloseButton && ( } > Close )} ) } ``` **Files:** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/dialog.tsx` (lines 42-79) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/select.tsx` (lines 50-55) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/combobox.tsx` (lines 39-49, 74) #### Pattern 7: Indicator/Icon Components Sub-components for visual indicators within compound structures: ```typescript function CheckboxPrimitive.Root { } ``` **Files:** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/checkbox.tsx` (lines 8-26) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/radio-group.tsx` (lines 28-33) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` (lines 176-183) ### TypeScript Patterns #### 1. **Type Extraction from Primitives** ```typescript // Extract types directly from the primitive library function AccordionItem({ className, ...props }: AccordionPrimitive.Item.Props) { return } // Combine with custom types function SelectTrigger({ className, size = "default", children, ...props }: SelectPrimitive.Trigger.Props & { size?: "sm" | "default" }) { return } // Extend with CVA variants function Button({ className, variant = "default", size = "default", ...props }: ButtonPrimitive.Props & VariantProps) { return } ``` #### 2. **React.ComponentProps for HTML Elements** For styled wrapper divs: ```typescript function DialogHeader({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return (
) } function AlertDialogMedia({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { return (
) } ``` #### 3. **React.ComponentPropsWithRef for Forward References** ```typescript function NavigationMenuList({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentPropsWithRef) { return ( ) } ``` **File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/navigation-menu.tsx` (lines 29-42) ### Shared Utility Function **File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/templates/start-app/src/lib/utils.ts` ```typescript import { clsx } from "clsx" import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge" import type { ClassValue } from "clsx" export function cn(...inputs: Array) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)) } ``` This utility: - Uses `clsx` to handle conditional classes - Uses `tailwind-merge` to intelligently merge conflicting Tailwind classes - Allows passing arrays, objects, booleans, and strings seamlessly ### Complex Component Example: Select Component **File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/select.tsx` This 200+ line component demonstrates all major patterns: 1. **Root Export:** `const Select = SelectPrimitive.Root` (line 9) 2. **Group Component:** `SelectGroup` wraps `SelectPrimitive.Group` (lines 11-19) 3. **Trigger with Props:** `SelectTrigger` accepts size variant (lines 31-57) 4. **Content with Positioning:** `SelectContent` uses Portal + Positioner pattern (lines 59-99) 5. **List Components:** `SelectLabel`, `SelectItem` with variants (lines 101-141) 6. **Scroll Buttons:** `SelectScrollUpButton`, `SelectScrollDownButton` (lines 155-189) 7. **Indicator Pattern:** Uses `SelectPrimitive.ItemIndicator` with render prop (lines 131-137) ### Export Patterns All components export their sub-component functions as named exports: ```typescript export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent } export { Dialog, DialogClose, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogOverlay, DialogPortal, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger, } export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants // Sometimes CVA variants are exported } ``` ### Component Organization by Complexity **Simple Components (10-30 lines):** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/collapsible.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/checkbox.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/radio-group.tsx` **Medium Components (30-80 lines):** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/accordion.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/tabs.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/dialog.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/popover.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/tooltip.tsx` **Complex Components (80-300 lines):** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/select.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/combobox.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/navigation-menu.tsx` - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504564704-dwzo3r/apps/v4/styles/base-vega/ui/alert-dialog.tsx` ### State Management Approach The library does **NOT** use React Context in the wrapped components. State management is delegated entirely to the @base-ui/react primitives, which handle: - Open/closed states via attribute selectors (`data-open`, `data-closed`) - Selected states (`data-checked`, `data-active`, `aria-expanded`) - Disabled states (`aria-disabled`, `disabled`) - Orientation (`data-horizontal`, `data-vertical`, `data-orientation`) Child components respond to parent state through CSS group selectors and data attributes, not React Context. ### CSS Architecture **Scoped Group Selectors with Data Attributes:** ``` group/tabs → Parent scope for Tab component group-data-horizontal/tabs → Responds to parent's data-horizontal group-data-vertical/tabs → Responds to parent's data-vertical group-data-[variant=line]/tabs → Responds to variant data attribute ``` **Example from Tabs:** ``` data-horizontal:flex-col → If parent has data-horizontal, flex-col group-data-vertical/tabs:w-full → If parent has data-vertical, full width group-data-vertical/tabs:justify-start → Justify start in vertical mode ``` ### Key Conventions Summary | Aspect | Convention | |--------|-----------| | **File naming** | `kebab-case.tsx` (e.g., `alert-dialog.tsx`) | | **Component naming** | PascalCase (e.g., `AccordionTrigger`) | | **Root component** | Often named after file or directly aliased from primitive | | **Sub-components** | `{RootName}{SubType}` (e.g., `AccordionTrigger`) | | **Props spreading** | Always destructure `{ className, ...props }` | | **Data attributes** | `data-slot="{kebab-case-name}"` on all elements | | **CSS utility** | `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for class merging | | **Styling approach** | Tailwind CSS with scoped group selectors | | **Variant system** | CVA (class-variance-authority) for complex variants | | **Client components** | `"use client"` directive for interactive components | | **Primitive wrapper** | Direct pass-through of primitive props with added styling | This architecture creates highly composable, accessible components that scale from simple to complex use cases while maintaining consistency across the entire UI library.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in the shadcn/ui component library. Here's all the context:

## Codebase Structure

Components live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` (e.g., accordion.tsx, tabs.tsx, carousel.tsx)
Examples live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` (e.g., accordion-demo.tsx, tabs-demo.tsx)
UI registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (array of registry items)
Examples registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (array of example items)

## Key Conventions from Existing Components

1. **File header**: `"use client"` directive, imports `* as React` from "react", imports `cn` from `@/lib/utils`
2. **No forwardRef**: Components use plain function declarations, NOT forwardRef
3. **Props**: Use `React.ComponentProps<"div">` for HTML elements, or extend with `& { customProp?: type }`
4. **data-slot**: Every component has `data-slot="kebab-case-name"` attribute
5. **cn()**: Always destructure `className` and merge via `cn(defaultClasses, className)`
6. **Exports**: Named exports at bottom of file in an `export { ... }` block
7. **No default exports** in component files (only in example/demo files)

## React Context Pattern (from carousel.tsx)

The Carousel component is the best reference for custom context:
```tsx
type CarouselContextProps = {
  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  // ...
} & CarouselProps

const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

function useCarousel() {
  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
  }
  return context
}
```
Root component wraps children in `<CarouselContext.Provider value={...}>`.
Sub-components call `useCarousel()` to access shared state.

## Orientation Pattern (from tabs.tsx)

Tabs handles orientation with data attributes + group selectors:
```tsx
function Tabs({ orientation = "horizontal", className, ...props }) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="tabs"
      data-orientation={orientation}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn("group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```
Child components use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:...` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:...` Tailwind classes.

## Registry Entry Format

UI component entry:
```ts
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }],
}
```

Example entry:
```ts
{
  name: "stepper-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["stepper"],
  files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }],
}
```

## Requirements

Create a "Stepper" compound component with these sub-components:
- **Stepper** (root container) - manages active step state, orientation
- **StepperItem** - individual step wrapper (holds step value/index)
- **StepperTrigger** - clickable area to navigate to a step
- **StepperTitle** - title text for a step
- **StepperDescription** - description text for a step
- **StepperSeparator** - visual connector line between steps
- **StepperContent** - content panel shown for the active step

Support:
- Horizontal and vertical orientations
- React context to share activeStep, orientation between sub-components
- Step states: completed, active, upcoming (via data attributes)
- Controlled and uncontrolled modes (value/onValueChange + defaultValue)

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Only create NEW files and ADD entries to registries
- Follow all conventions above exactly

Please provide:
1. The exact file paths to create
2. The exact registry entries to add (and where in the file)
3. A detailed design of the stepper.tsx component (all types, context, sub-components with their props, styling approach, state management)
4. A detailed design of the stepper-demo.tsx example
5. Any edge cases or design decisions to call out

Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in the shadcn/ui component library. Here's all the context:

## Codebase Structure

Components live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` (e.g., accordion.tsx, tabs.tsx, carousel.tsx)
Examples live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` (e.g., accordion-demo.tsx, tabs-demo.tsx)
UI registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (array of registry items)
Examples registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (array of example items)

## Key Conventions from Existing Components

1. **File header**: `"use client"` directive, imports `* as React` from "react", imports `cn` from `@/lib/utils`
2. **No forwardRef**: Components use plain function declarations, NOT forwardRef
3. **Props**: Use `React.ComponentProps<"div">` for HTML elements, or extend with `& { customProp?: type }`
4. **data-slot**: Every component has `data-slot="kebab-case-name"` attribute
5. **cn()**: Always destructure `className` and merge via `cn(defaultClasses, className)`
6. **Exports**: Named exports at bottom of file in an `export { ... }` block
7. **No default exports** in component files (only in example/demo files)

## React Context Pattern (from carousel.tsx)

The Carousel component is the best reference for custom context:
```tsx
type CarouselContextProps = {
  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  // ...
} & CarouselProps

const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

function useCarousel() {
  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
  }
  return context
}
```
Root component wraps children in `<CarouselContext.Provider value={...}>`.
Sub-components call `useCarousel()` to access shared state.

## Orientation Pattern (from tabs.tsx)

Tabs handles orientation with data attributes + group selectors:
```tsx
function Tabs({ orientation = "horizontal", className, ...props }) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="tabs"
      data-orientation={orientation}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn("group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```
Child components use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:...` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:...` Tailwind classes.

## Registry Entry Format

UI component entry:
```ts
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }],
}

{
  name: "stepper-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["stepper"],
  files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }],
}
```

3	import * as React from "react"
4	import { type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
5	import { ToggleGroup as ToggleGroupPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
6	
7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
8	import { toggleVariants } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle"
9	
10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(
68	        toggleVariants({
69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )
81	}
82	
83	export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in the shadcn/ui component library. Here's all the context:

## Codebase Structure

Components live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` (e.g., accordion.tsx, tabs.tsx, carousel.tsx)
Examples live at: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` (e.g., accordion-demo.tsx, tabs-demo.tsx)
UI registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (array of registry items)
Examples registry: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (array of example items)

## Key Conventions from Existing Components

1. **File header**: `"use client"` directive, imports `* as React` from "react", imports `cn` from `@/lib/utils`
2. **No forwardRef**: Components use plain function declarations, NOT forwardRef
3. **Props**: Use `React.ComponentProps<"div">` for HTML elements, or extend with `& { customProp?: type }`
4. **data-slot**: Every component has `data-slot="kebab-case-name"` attribute
5. **cn()**: Always destructure `className` and merge via `cn(defaultClasses, className)`
6. **Exports**: Named exports at bottom of file in an `export { ... }` block
7. **No default exports** in component files (only in example/demo files)

## React Context Pattern (from carousel.tsx)

The Carousel component is the best reference for custom context:
```tsx
type CarouselContextProps = {
  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  // ...
} & CarouselProps

const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

function useCarousel() {
  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
  }
  return context
}
```
Root component wraps children in `<CarouselContext.Provider value={...}>`.
Sub-components call `useCarousel()` to access shared state.

## Orientation Pattern (from tabs.tsx)

Tabs handles orientation with data attributes + group selectors:
```tsx
function Tabs({ orientation = "horizontal", className, ...props }) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="tabs"
      data-orientation={orientation}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn("group/tabs flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```
Child components use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:...` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:...` Tailwind classes.

## Registry Entry Format

UI component entry:
```ts
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }],
}
```

Example entry:
```ts
{
  name: "stepper-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["stepper"],
  files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }],
}
```

## Requirements

Create a "Stepper" compound component with these sub-components:
- **Stepper** (root container) - manages active step state, orientation
- **StepperItem** - individual step wrapper (holds step value/index)
- **StepperTrigger** - clickable area to navigate to a step
- **StepperTitle** - title text for a step
- **StepperDescription** - description text for a step
- **StepperSeparator** - visual connector line between steps
- **StepperContent** - content panel shown for the active step

Support:
- Horizontal and vertical orientations
- React context to share activeStep, orientation between sub-components
- Step states: completed, active, upcoming (via data attributes)
- Controlled and uncontrolled modes (value/onValueChange + defaultValue)

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Only create NEW files and ADD entries to registries
- Follow all conventions above exactly

Please provide:
1. The exact file paths to create
2. The exact registry entries to add (and where in the file)
3. A detailed design of the stepper.tsx component (all types, context, sub-components with their props, styling approach, state management)
4. A detailed design of the stepper-demo.tsx example
5. Any edge cases or design decisions to call out

content.text: Excellent. I have a comprehensive understanding now. Let me write the final plan.

input.content:
# Stepper Component Implementation Plan

## Context

The shadcn/ui component library needs a **Stepper** compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. No stepper exists today. The component will be built from scratch using React context (no Radix primitive — same approach as Carousel), following all existing conventions: `data-slot` attributes, `cn()` class merging, `"use client"` directive, named exports, and orientation via `data-orientation` + Tailwind group selectors.

## Files to Create

1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** — the compound component
2. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** — the demo example

## Files to Modify (append-only — no existing code changes)

3. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** — add stepper entry after `spinner` (alphabetical)
4. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`** — add stepper-demo entry (alphabetical among "s" entries)

---

## Step 1: Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`

### Architecture

Two React contexts:
- **`StepperContext`** — root-level: `activeStep`, `setActiveStep`, `orientation`, `linear`
- **`StepperItemContext`** — item-level: `step` number, computed `state` (`"completed"` | `"active"` | `"upcoming"`)

### Sub-components

| Component | Element | data-slot | Key behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Stepper` | `<div>` | `stepper` | Root. Manages active step state (controlled/uncontrolled). Provides `StepperContext`. `group/stepper`. |
| `StepperItem` | `<div>` | `stepper-item` | Wraps one step. Registers step number. Provides `StepperItemContext`. Exposes `data-state`. |
| `StepperTrigger` | `<button>` | `stepper-trigger` | Clickable circle. Shows step number or `CheckIcon` when completed. Respects `linear` prop. |
| `StepperTitle` | `<div>` | `stepper-title` | Step label. Colors based on `data-state`. |
| `StepperDescription` | `<div>` | `stepper-description` | Step description. Muted colors based on `data-state`. |
| `StepperSeparator` | `<div>` | `stepper-separator` | Connector line. Orientation-aware sizing. Fills with `bg-primary` when completed. |
| `StepperContent` | `<div>` | `stepper-content` | Content panel. Only renders when its parent step is active. |

### Props

```
Stepper: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & {
  defaultValue?: number        // uncontrolled initial step (default: 1)
  value?: number               // controlled active step
  onValueChange?: (value: number) => void
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"  // default: "horizontal"
  linear?: boolean             // restrict forward-only navigation (default: false)
}

StepperItem: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & {
  step: number                 // required step identifier
}

StepperTrigger: React.ComponentProps<"button">  // default content: CheckIcon if completed, step number otherwise
StepperTitle: React.ComponentProps<"div">
StepperDescription: React.ComponentProps<"div">
StepperSeparator: React.ComponentProps<"div">
StepperContent: React.ComponentProps<"div">
```

### State management

- Controlled/uncontrolled pattern: `const activeStep = valueProp ?? _activeStep` (same as Carousel/SidebarProvider)
- Step registration via `useEffect` in `StepperItem` calling `registerStep`/`unregisterStep` on context
- Step state derived: `activeStep > step → "completed"`, `=== → "active"`, `< → "upcoming"`

### Styling approach

- Orientation: `data-[orientation=horizontal]` / `data-[orientation=vertical]` on root and separator
- Step state: `data-[state=completed]` / `data-[state=active]` / `data-[state=upcoming]` on trigger, title, description, separator
- Trigger: `size-8 rounded-full border` circle; primary bg when active/completed; muted border when upcoming
- Separator: `h-0.5 w-full flex-1` horizontal; `w-0.5 min-h-4` vertical; fills primary when completed
- Focus: `focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50` (matches tabs/accordion)

### Exports

```tsx
export {
  Stepper,
  StepperItem,
  StepperTrigger,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperContent,
  useStepper,
}
```

### Key reference files (do NOT modify)
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx` — React context + orientation pattern
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx` — orientation data-attribute + group selector pattern
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx` — compound component conventions
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx` — orientation-aware separator styling

---

## Step 2: Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`

A simple 3-step horizontal stepper with `defaultValue={2}` so all three states are visible:
- Step 1: "Account" / "Create your account" → completed (shows CheckIcon)
- Step 2: "Profile" / "Set up your profile" → active (highlighted)
- Step 3: "Complete" / "Review and confirm" → upcoming (muted)

Imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper`. Uses `export default function StepperDemo()`. No `"use client"` directive (demos don't need it — the component file has it).

---

## Step 3: Add registry entry to `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`

Insert after the `spinner` entry (after line 563), before `switch`:

```ts
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["lucide-react"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
},
```

---

## Step 4: Add registry entry to `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`

Insert in alphabetical order among "s" entries:

```ts
{
  name: "stepper-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["stepper"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
},
```

---

## Verification

1. **TypeScript**: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` from `apps/v4/` to verify no type errors
2. **Build registry**: Run the registry build script (`pnpm build:registry` or equivalent) to confirm the new entries are picked up in the generated `__index__.tsx` files
3. **Visual**: Load the stepper-demo in the app's example viewer to confirm:
   - 3 steps render horizontally with separators between them
   - Step 1 shows a check icon (completed), step 2 is highlighted (active), step 3 is muted (upcoming)
   - Clicking step 3 navigates to it; clicking step 1 goes back
   - Separator between step 1-2 is filled (primary color)

No existing component files are modified.

## Context

The shadcn/ui component library needs a **Stepper** compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. No stepper exists today. The component will be built from scratch using React context (no Radix primitive — same approach as Carousel), following all existing conventions: `data-slot` attributes, `cn()` class merging, `"use client"` directive, named exports, and orientation via `data-orientation` + Tailwind group selectors.

## Files to Create

1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** — the compound component
2. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** — the demo example

## Files to Modify (append-only — no existing code changes)

3. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** — add stepper entry after `spinner` (alphabetical)
4. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`** — add stepper-demo entry (alphabetical among "s" entries)

---

## Step 1: Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`

### Architecture

Two React contexts:
- **`StepperContext`** — root-level: `activeStep`, `setActiveStep`, `orientation`, `linear`
- **`StepperItemContext`** — item-level: `step` number, computed `state` (`"completed"` | `"active"` | `"upcoming"`)

### Sub-components

| Component | Element | data-slot | Key behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Stepper` | `<div>` | `stepper` | Root. Manages active step state (controlled/uncontrolled). Provides `StepperContext`. `group/stepper`. |
| `StepperItem` | `<div>` | `stepper-item` | Wraps one step. Registers step number. Provides `StepperItemContext`. Exposes `data-state`. |
| `StepperTrigger` | `<button>` | `stepper-trigger` | Clickable circle. Shows step number or `CheckIcon` when completed. Respects `linear` prop. |
| `StepperTitle` | `<div>` | `stepper-title` | Step label. Colors based on `data-state`. |
| `StepperDescription` | `<div>` | `stepper-description` | Step description. Muted colors based on `data-state`. |
| `StepperSeparator` | `<div>` | `stepper-separator` | Connector line. Orientation-aware sizing. Fills with `bg-primary` when completed. |
| `StepperContent` | `<div>` | `stepper-content` | Content panel. Only renders when its parent step is active. |

### Props

```
Stepper: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & {
  defaultValue?: number        // uncontrolled initial step (default: 1)
  value?: number               // controlled active step
  onValueChange?: (value: number) => void
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"  // default: "horizontal"
  linear?: boolean             // restrict forward-only navigation (default: false)
}

StepperItem: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & {
  step: number                 // required step identifier
}

StepperTrigger: React.ComponentProps<"button">  // default content: CheckIcon if completed, step number otherwise
StepperTitle: React.ComponentProps<"div">
StepperDescription: React.ComponentProps<"div">
StepperSeparator: React.ComponentProps<"div">
StepperContent: React.ComponentProps<"div">
```

### State management

- Controlled/uncontrolled pattern: `const activeStep = valueProp ?? _activeStep` (same as Carousel/SidebarProvider)
- Step registration via `useEffect` in `StepperItem` calling `registerStep`/`unregisterStep` on context
- Step state derived: `activeStep > step → "completed"`, `=== → "active"`, `< → "upcoming"`

### Styling approach

- Orientation: `data-[orientation=horizontal]` / `data-[orientation=vertical]` on root and separator
- Step state: `data-[state=completed]` / `data-[state=active]` / `data-[state=upcoming]` on trigger, title, description, separator
- Trigger: `size-8 rounded-full border` circle; primary bg when active/completed; muted border when upcoming
- Separator: `h-0.5 w-full flex-1` horizontal; `w-0.5 min-h-4` vertical; fills primary when completed
- Focus: `focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50` (matches tabs/accordion)

### Exports

```tsx
export {
  Stepper,
  StepperItem,
  StepperTrigger,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperContent,
  useStepper,
}
```

### Key reference files (do NOT modify)
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx` — React context + orientation pattern
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx` — orientation data-attribute + group selector pattern
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx` — compound component conventions
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx` — orientation-aware separator styling

---

## Step 2: Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`

A simple 3-step horizontal stepper with `defaultValue={2}` so all three states are visible:
- Step 1: "Account" / "Create your account" → completed (shows CheckIcon)
- Step 2: "Profile" / "Set up your profile" → active (highlighted)
- Step 3: "Complete" / "Review and confirm" → upcoming (muted)

Imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper`. Uses `export default function StepperDemo()`. No `"use client"` directive (demos don't need it — the component file has it).

---

## Step 3: Add registry entry to `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`

Insert after the `spinner` entry (after line 563), before `switch`:

```ts
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["lucide-react"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
},
```

---

## Step 4: Add registry entry to `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`

Insert in alphabetical order among "s" entries:

```ts
{
  name: "stepper-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["stepper"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
},
```

---

## Verification

1. **TypeScript**: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` from `apps/v4/` to verify no type errors
2. **Build registry**: Run the registry build script (`pnpm build:registry` or equivalent) to confirm the new entries are picked up in the generated `__index__.tsx` files
3. **Visual**: Load the stepper-demo in the app's example viewer to confirm:
   - 3 steps render horizontally with separators between them
   - Step 1 shows a check icon (completed), step 2 is highlighted (active), step 3 is muted (upcoming)
   - Clicking step 3 navigates to it; clicking step 1 goes back
   - Separator between step 1-2 is filled (primary color)

1	export * from "../registry/schema"
2

1	import { z } from "zod"
2	
3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>

1	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
2	import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
3	
4	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
5	import { Separator } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator"
6	
7	const buttonGroupVariants = cva(
8	  "flex w-fit items-stretch has-[>[data-slot=button-group]]:gap-2 [&>*]:focus-visible:relative [&>*]:focus-visible:z-10 has-[select[aria-hidden=true]:last-child]:[&>[data-slot=select-trigger]:last-of-type]:rounded-r-md [&>[data-slot=select-trigger]:not([class*='w-'])]:w-fit [&>input]:flex-1",
9	  {
10	    variants: {
11	      orientation: {
12	        horizontal:
13	          "[&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-l-none [&>*:not(:first-child)]:border-l-0 [&>*:not(:last-child)]:rounded-r-none",
14	        vertical:
15	          "flex-col [&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-t-none [&>*:not(:first-child)]:border-t-0 [&>*:not(:last-child)]:rounded-b-none",
16	      },
17	    },
18	    defaultVariants: {
19	      orientation: "horizontal",
20	    },
21	  }
22	)
23	
24	function ButtonGroup({
25	  className,
26	  orientation,
27	  ...props
28	}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & VariantProps<typeof buttonGroupVariants>) {
29	  return (
30	    <div
31	      role="group"
32	      data-slot="button-group"
33	      data-orientation={orientation}
34	      className={cn(buttonGroupVariants({ orientation }), className)}
35	      {...props}
36	    />
37	  )
38	}
39	
40	function ButtonGroupText({
41	  className,
42	  asChild = false,
43	  ...props
44	}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & {
45	  asChild?: boolean
46	}) {
47	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "div"
48	
49	  return (
50	    <Comp
51	      className={cn(
52	        "flex items-center gap-2 rounded-md border bg-muted px-4 text-sm font-medium shadow-xs [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
53	        className
54	      )}
55	      {...props}
56	    />
57	  )
58	}
59	
60	function ButtonGroupSeparator({
61	  className,
62	  orientation = "vertical",
63	  ...props
64	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof Separator>) {
65	  return (
66	    <Separator
67	      data-slot="button-group-separator"
68	      orientation={orientation}
69	      className={cn(
70	        "relative m-0! self-stretch bg-input data-[orientation=vertical]:h-auto",
71	        className
72	      )}
73	      {...props}
74	    />
75	  )
76	}
77	
78	export {
79	  ButtonGroup,
80	  ButtonGroupSeparator,
81	  ButtonGroupText,
82	  buttonGroupVariants,
83	}

1	# shadcn
2	
3	A CLI for adding components to your project.
4	
5	## create
6	
7	Use the `create` command to create a new project. You will be taken to a website to build your custom design system and choose your framework.
8	
9	```bash
10	npx shadcn create
11	```
12	
13	## init
14	
15	Use the `init` command to initialize dependencies for a new project.
16	
17	The `init` command installs dependencies, adds the `cn` util, configures Tailwind CSS, and CSS variables for the project.
18	
19	```bash
20	npx shadcn init
21	```
22	
23	## add
24	
25	Use the `add` command to add components to your project.
26	
27	The `add` command adds a component to your project and installs all required dependencies.
28	
29	```bash
30	npx shadcn add [component]
31	```
32	
33	### Example
34	
35	```bash
36	npx shadcn add alert-dialog
37	```
38	
39	You can also run the command without any arguments to view a list of all available components:
40	
41	```bash
42	npx shadcn add
43	```
44	
45	## Documentation
46	
47	Visit https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli to view the documentation.
48	
49	## License
50	
51	Licensed under the [MIT license](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/LICENSE.md).
52

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary report. ## Comprehensive shadcn/ui Component Library Exploration Report Based on my thorough analysis of the codebase, here's everything you need to know to add a Stepper component: --- ## 1. Overall Project Structure **Root Directory Structure:** ``` ├── apps/v4/ # Main Next.js documentation app ├── packages/shadcn/ # The shadcn/ui component library package ├── templates/ # Project templates ├── scripts/ # Build and utility scripts ├── .github/ # GitHub workflows └── pnpm-workspace.yaml # Monorepo configuration ``` **Key Files:** - `/package.json` - Root monorepo package - `/pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Defines workspaces (apps/v4 and packages/shadcn) - `/turbo.json` - Turborepo configuration - `/tsconfig.json` - Root TypeScript config --- ## 2. Component Location and Structure **Primary Component Directory:** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` - Where all UI components live **Example Component Files:** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx` (lines 1-67) - Simple compound component - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx` (lines 1-92) - Compound with variants - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx` (lines 1-242) - Complex with React Context - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog.tsx` (lines 1-159) - Large compound component - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/collapsible.tsx` (lines 1-34) - Minimal wrapper - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/popover.tsx` - Helper functions pattern **Supporting Directories:** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` - Component usage examples - `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/` - MDX documentation files - `/apps/v4/lib/` - Utilities (cn function at `/apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`) --- ## 3. Registry System - How It Works **Registry Entry Points:** 1. **UI Component Registry:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - Contains all `registry:ui` type items - Each component entry follows this format: ```typescript { name: "accordion", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], registryDependencies: ["button"], // For components that depend on other shadcn components files: [ { path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, ``` 2. **Examples Registry:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Contains all `registry:example` type items - Format: ```typescript { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, ``` 3. **Main Registry Assembly:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` - Combines all registries (ui, blocks, charts, lib, hooks, themes, examples, internal) - Uses Zod validation via `registryItemSchema` - Filters out deprecated items 4. **Built Registry Output:** `/apps/v4/registry.json` - Generated JSON file containing all component metadata - Built by running: `pnpm registry:build` **Registry Schema Definition:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts` - Defines `RegistryItem` type with discriminated union - Key fields: - `name` - Component identifier - `type` - registry:ui, registry:example, registry:hook, etc. - `dependencies` - npm packages (e.g., "radix-ui") - `registryDependencies` - Other shadcn components - `files` - Array of file objects with path and type - `tailwind` - Tailwind config (optional) - `cssVars` - CSS variables (optional) - `css` - Raw CSS to add (optional) - `devDependencies` - Dev packages --- ## 4. Component File Structure Patterns ### Pattern A: Simple Wrapper (Collapsible) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/collapsible.tsx` ```typescript "use client" import { Collapsible as CollapsiblePrimitive } from "radix-ui" function Collapsible({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return } function CollapsibleTrigger({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return ( ) } function CollapsibleContent({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return ( ) } export { Collapsible, CollapsibleTrigger, CollapsibleContent } ``` **Key Patterns:** - `"use client"` directive at top - Minimal styling - just wraps Radix primitive - All exports named as compound component parts - Uses `data-slot` attribute for CSS targeting ### Pattern B: Styled Compound (Accordion) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx` ```typescript "use client" import * as React from "react" import { ChevronDownIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Accordion as AccordionPrimitive } from "radix-ui" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" function Accordion({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return } function AccordionItem({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return ( ) } function AccordionTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return ( {children} ) } function AccordionContent({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return (
{children}
 ) } export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent } ``` **Key Patterns:** - Import lucide-react icons - Use `cn()` utility for merging Tailwind classes - Comprehensive styling with data attributes for state - Helper components (Header) for semantic HTML - Tailwind animation classes ### Pattern C: With Variants (Tabs) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx` ```typescript import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority" import { Tabs as TabsPrimitive } from "radix-ui" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" const tabsListVariants = cva( "group/tabs-list inline-flex w-fit items-center justify-center...", { variants: { variant: { default: "bg-muted", line: "gap-1 bg-transparent", }, }, defaultVariants: { variant: "default", }, } ) function TabsList({ className, variant = "default", ...props }: React.ComponentProps & VariantProps) { return ( ) } export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants } ``` **Key Patterns:** - Uses `class-variance-authority` (CVA) for variant management - Exports variants alongside components - `VariantProps` for TypeScript ### Pattern D: With React Context (Carousel) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx` (lines 1-43) ```typescript "use client" import * as React from "react" import useEmblaCarousel, { type UseEmblaCarouselType } from "embla-carousel-react" type CarouselApi = UseEmblaCarouselType[1] type CarouselContextProps = { carouselRef: ReturnType[0] api: ReturnType[1] scrollPrev: () => void scrollNext: () => void canScrollPrev: boolean canScrollNext: boolean } & CarouselProps const CarouselContext = React.createContext(null) function useCarousel() { const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext) if (!context) { throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a ") } return context } function Carousel({ orientation = "horizontal", opts, setApi, plugins, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & CarouselProps) { const [carouselRef, api] = useEmblaCarousel({ ... }) const [canScrollPrev, setCanScrollPrev] = React.useState(false) const [canScrollNext, setCanScrollNext] = React.useState(false) // ... handlers and effects ... return (
{children}
) } function CarouselContent({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { const { carouselRef, orientation } = useCarousel() // ... use context values ... } export { type CarouselApi, Carousel, CarouselContent, CarouselItem, ... } ``` **Context Pattern:** - Define TypeScript types for context value - Create context with `React.createContext(null)` - Create custom hook `useContextName()` that throws error if null - Use context in child components via hook - Export types alongside components --- ## 5. Our flagship product combines cutting-edge technology with sleek design. Built with premium materials, it offers unparalleled performance and reliability.

Key features include advanced processing capabilities, and an intuitive user interface designed for both beginners and experts.

Shipping Details

We offer worldwide shipping through trusted courier partners. Standard delivery takes 3-5 business days, while express shipping ensures delivery within 1-2 business days.

All orders are carefully packaged and fully insured. Track your shipment in real-time through our dedicated tracking portal.

Return Policy

We stand behind our products with a comprehensive 30-day return policy. If you're not completely satisfied, simply return the item in its original condition.

Our hassle-free return process includes free return shipping and full refunds processed within 48 hours of receiving the returned item.

Everything looks good. Here's a summary of what was created and modified: ## Files Created ### 1. `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` — The Stepper component A compound component with **two levels of React context**: - **`StepperContext`** — Shared by the root ``, provides `activeStep`, `orientation`, and `onStepChange` to all descendants - **`StepItemContext`** — Provided by each ``, gives sub-components (`StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, etc.) access to that step's `step` index, `isActive`, `isCompleted`, and `isDisabled` state **Sub-components:** | Component | Purpose | |---|---| | `Stepper` | Root container — accepts `activeStep`, `orientation` ("horizontal"/"vertical"), `onStepChange` | | `StepperItem` | Wraps an individual step — accepts `step` (index) and optional `disabled` | | `StepperTrigger` | Clickable button that calls `onStepChange(step)` | | `StepperIndicator` | Circular step number badge (shows ✓ when completed) | | `StepperTitle` | Step heading (`

`) | | `StepperDescription` | Step subtitle (`

`) | | `StepperSeparator` | Line between steps (horizontal or vertical, fills with `bg-primary` when completed) | | `StepperContent` | Content panel — only renders when its step is active | | `useStepper` | Custom hook to access stepper context | Follows all existing patterns: `"use client"`, `data-slot` attributes, `data-state` attributes, `cn()` utility, `React.ComponentProps<>` typing, named function exports. ### 2. `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` — Demo example A 3-step onboarding wizard (Account → Profile → Complete) with Previous/Next buttons, showing the horizontal stepper with clickable triggers, indicators, titles, descriptions, and separators. ## Files Modified (registry entries only) ### 3. `registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` Added `stepper` entry (alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`). ### 4. `registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` Added `stepper-demo` entry (alphabetically between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo`) with `registryDependencies: ["stepper"]`.
